title: tragopogon brevirostris subsp. longifolius
intro: Tragopogon brevirostris subsp. longifolius, also known as salsify or goat's beard, is a plant species in the sunflower family characterized by its yellow flowers and edible roots. The medicinal properties of Tragopogon brevirostris subsp. longifolius include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of consuming Tragopogon brevirostris subsp. longifolius include its potential to support digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and provide essential vitamins and minerals. Herbal preparations of Tragopogon brevirostris subsp. longifolius can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
